agent1_raw: The mAP improvement on 1,847 nighttime frames is a useful detector quality signal, but the board's nighttime braking safety claim requires different evidence. The planner requires stable 400ms detections at minimum stopping distance — per-frame COCO mAP measures neither stability nor timing. The 38ms inference time on the production SoC is also not reflected in the headline metric and could affect lead time. Planner-in-the-loop testing is required.
agent2_raw: Three gaps: (1) metric mismatch — mAP is a detector metric, the claim is a braking safety claim; (2) temporal persistence — the 400ms planner trigger is not measured by per-frame metrics; (3) latency penalty — 38ms vs 24ms is a real-world operational change not captured in mAP. Same-hardware evaluation is not a flaw; it's the right controlled comparison. But planner-in-the-loop testing is still needed.
agent3_raw: The board cannot cite the per-frame mAP result in a nighttime emergency braking safety statement. The missing evidence is planner-in-the-loop or closed-track testing measuring braking success rate. The 400ms persistence requirement and the 38ms vs 24ms latency difference are both relevant to whether the mAP improvement actually helps braking outcomes.
synthesis_raw: All three agents agree on empirical_test_agreed. The three issues — metric mismatch, temporal persistence gap, and latency — are consistently identified. None incorrectly challenges the identical camera hardware evaluation or the 1847-frame nighttime slice as invalid. ETD: planner-in-the-loop closed-track testing; supports_critique_if = no improvement or worse braking outcomes; supports_defense_if = significant improvement in braking success and stopping margin; ambiguous_if = selective improvements without clear safety net.
all_issues_raised: Metric mismatch: detector mAP vs nighttime braking safety claim
Per-frame mAP does not measure 400ms temporal persistence requirement
38ms vs 24ms inference latency on production SoC not in headline metric

empirical_test.supports_critique_if: No improvement or worse braking outcomes due to temporal persistence and latency effects
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Significant improvement in nighttime braking success and stopping margin despite slower runtime
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Selective improvements that leave overall safety impact unclear
